entities.name: Fitbit API
entities.context: Health data API being queried for intraday vitals and daily activity summaries

entities.name: Workout Tracker
entities.context: Internal system component (workout_tracker) used for syncing intraday health data

entities.name: DuckDB/SQLite
entities.context: Database connection (conn) used to store synced health metrics

entities.name: Intraday Vitals Sync
entities.context: Process that syncs intraday health metrics, limited to last 48 hours to conserve API quota

entities.name: Daily Activity Summary
entities.context: Aggregated daily metrics including steps, floors, elevation, calories, and activity minutes

entities.name: API Rate Limiting
entities.context: HTTP 429 handling with graceful degradation — errors skip individual dates rather than aborting entire sync

entities.name: Health Data Pipeline
entities.context: Multi-stage sync pipeline: intraday metrics (2-day window) then daily summaries over configurable range

relationships.context: Pipeline fetches data from Fitbit API endpoints (ACTIVITIES_DATE_URL) using OAuth token
_sync_intraday_for_date delegates to workout_tracker for intraday vitals storage
Database connection passed through pipeline for persisting synced health data
48-hour window explicitly designed to save API quota
Per-date fetches wrapped in try/except to handle 429 errors gracefully
First stage of pipeline — runs before daily summary sync
Second stage of pipeline — iterates over configurable date range
